explanation_box.text: This reptile likely is considered average fertile because it camouflages well in it's habitat, which would ensure it an it's offspring's survival.
This reptile's fertility score is average because it is able to camouflage with it's environment, ensuring it's and it's offspring's survival.
This reptile is very fertile because it has bold colors which attract many mates, therefore it can select the strongest mate, passing on superior genes to ensure survival in its offspring.
This reptile is still very fertile because it is brightly colored, attracting mates. It can pick the best suitor which will pass on superior traits to the offspring, ensuring survival.
This reptile received an average score because there was a lack of suitable mates to choose from for various reasons, this season, resulting in lower fertility than before.
The season rebounded and this reptile has a better suitor pool to choose from, better genes are passed on this season to the offspring ensuring successful fertility.
While this reptile is well camouflaged and tends to have an average fertility rate, the not vivid color could also result in not a great choice of suitors, therefore not passing on great genes.
This season this reptile had its best season, perhaps there were a lot more males with superior genes, that contributed to a higher fertility success.
There are now evolutionary adjustments being made to accommodate average fertility rates before, survival of the fittest is kicking in and the offspring are evolving in order to survive.
This reptile may have had a more stressful season and resulted in a lower fertility rate.

This reptile received this rating because it is getting to be older and has less of a success rate, it has attractive coloring to appeal to a mate, so internally it may not be the best set up for fertility.
This reptile could have mated with a strong suitor or it could be in it's egg laying prime which would result in stronger offspring.
This reptile could be at the end of it's egg laying years which would decrease the success of fertility.
This reptile has a bad season and could have stressors which led it to having a slightly above average fertility rating.
This reptile had a great season and found a strong mate, had access to food, water, and suffered very little street.
This reptile may not have had as much access to food or basic nutrients. It may not have been able to attract a strong mate.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?
